meanings.definition: (usually plural) a fragment scraped off of something and collected
examples: they collected blood scrapings for analysis

meanings.definition: a harsh noise made by scraping
examples: the scrape of violin bows distracted her

meanings.definition: a deep bow with the foot drawn backwards (indicating excessive humility)
examples: all that bowing and scraping did not impress him
The cat scraped at the armchair
They scraped a letter into the stone

meanings.definition: cut the surface of; wear away the surface of
bend the knees and bow in a servile manner
gather (money or other resources) together over time
examples: She had scraped together enough money for college

meanings.definition: bruise, cut, or injure the skin or the surface of
examples: The boy skinned his knee when he fell
We quarreled over the question as to who discovered America
These two fellows are always scrapping over something
scrap the old airplane and sell the parts
